title: Display status preserves per-fact freshness across adapter loss

preconditions.description: The harness holds the inherited device-keyed lease before any device or fixture access.
The harness recorded session generation, display, layout, lock state, ATX-observable state, browser identity, and fixture identity.

Snapshot and event caches contain deliberately unequal signal, resolution, and FPS observation times; the harness can pause exactly one call after its binding-checked RPC write and before its response.

The harness provisions each linked call in an independent case-owned ready session with a unique four-part adapter binding, request identifier, and separately captured fresh observation when required.

fault_script.action: For only status-with-unequal-facts, seed signal, resolution, and FPS cache records with deliberately unequal sources, observation times, ages, and freshness classifications.
fault_script.expected_effect: The one linked display-status read returns each fact with its own exact source, time, age, and freshness rather than a unified health timestamp.

fault_script.action: Unconditionally clear the unequal-fact seed injector after its one linked display-status call while retaining that call's returned evidence.
fault_script.expected_effect: No later call inherits the seed injector; the following binding-loss case begins from its separately configured qualified cache.

fault_script.action: Arm one-shot shared DeviceRpcAdapter binding loss for only session-status-after-binding-loss after its binding-checked read write and before its response.
fault_script.expected_effect: The qualified cache read returns explicit per-fact stale or unknown provenance, or a CONNECTION_LOST read envelope with outcome null, safe_to_retry true, and reconnect_then_capture guidance.

fault_script.action: Unconditionally clear the qualified-cache binding-loss injector, close its case-owned adapter and browser resources, and provision opaque-session-adapter-loss-display-capture generation 8 with a new four-part binding.
fault_script.expected_effect: No cache-read fault or old binding reaches the display-capture case.

fault_script.action: Arm one-shot binding replacement for only device-rpc-adapter-mid-flight-loss-jetkvm-display-capture after its binding-checked read write and before its response.
fault_script.expected_effect: The display-capture read returns CONNECTION_LOST with outcome null, safe_to_retry true, reconnect_then_capture guidance, and no mutation or replay.

fault_script.action: Unconditionally clear the display-capture loss injector, retire its generation-8 browser and adapter, restore the fixture baseline, and provision opaque-session-adapter-loss-display-status generation 9 with a new four-part binding.
fault_script.expected_effect: No display-capture fault, browser, channel, adapter, or generation reaches the display-status case.

fault_script.action: Arm one-shot binding replacement for only device-rpc-adapter-mid-flight-loss-jetkvm-display-status after its binding-checked read write and before its response.
fault_script.expected_effect: The display-status read returns independently qualified stale cached facts or CONNECTION_LOST with outcome null, safe_to_retry true, and reconnect_then_capture guidance.

fault_script.action: Unconditionally clear the display-status loss injector, retire its generation-9 browser and adapter, restore the fixture baseline, provision opaque-session-adapter-loss-input-keyboard generation 10 with a new four-part binding, and capture opaque-observation-device-rpc-adapter-mid-flight-loss-input-keyboard.
fault_script.expected_effect: No display-status fault or binding reaches the keyboard case, which begins ready with its own fresh observation.

fault_script.action: Arm one-shot binding replacement for only device-rpc-adapter-mid-flight-loss-jetkvm-input-keyboard after exactly one downstream action write and before acknowledgement.
fault_script.expected_effect: The keyboard mutation returns CONNECTION_LOST, unknown, safe_to_retry false, inspect_device_state_before_retry, dispatched_action_count 1, completed_action_count 0, and zero replay writes.

fault_script.action: Unconditionally clear the keyboard loss injector; inspect, emergency-release, and close its generation-10 case; restore the fixture baseline; provision opaque-session-adapter-loss-input-mouse generation 11 with a new four-part binding; and capture opaque-observation-device-rpc-adapter-mid-flight-loss-input-mouse.
fault_script.expected_effect: The closed generation-10 mutation gate and its observation are retired before the independent mouse case starts.

fault_script.action: Arm one-shot binding replacement for only device-rpc-adapter-mid-flight-loss-jetkvm-input-mouse after exactly one downstream action write and before acknowledgement.
fault_script.expected_effect: The mouse mutation returns CONNECTION_LOST, unknown, safe_to_retry false, inspect_device_state_before_retry, dispatched_action_count 1, completed_action_count 0, and zero replay writes.

fault_script.action: Unconditionally clear the mouse loss injector; inspect, emergency-release, and close its generation-11 case; restore the fixture baseline; provision opaque-session-adapter-loss-input-paste generation 12 with a new four-part binding; and capture opaque-observation-device-rpc-adapter-mid-flight-loss-input-paste.
fault_script.expected_effect: The closed generation-11 mutation gate and its observation are retired before the independent paste case starts.

fault_script.action: Arm one-shot binding replacement for only device-rpc-adapter-mid-flight-loss-jetkvm-input-paste after its first character event is dispatched and before any correlated completion.
fault_script.expected_effect: The paste mutation returns CONNECTION_LOST, unknown, safe_to_retry false, inspect_device_state_before_retry, dispatched_count 1, completed_count 0, thirteen suppressed suffix characters, and zero replay writes.

fault_script.action: Unconditionally clear the paste loss injector; inspect, emergency-release, stop and join the paste producer, and close its generation-12 case; restore the fixture baseline; and provision opaque-session-adapter-loss-input-release generation 13 with a new four-part binding.
fault_script.expected_effect: The closed generation-12 mutation gate, producer, and observation are retired before the independent release case starts.

fault_script.action: Arm one-shot binding replacement for only device-rpc-adapter-mid-flight-loss-jetkvm-input-release after exactly one downstream release write and before zero-state acknowledgement.
fault_script.expected_effect: The release mutation returns CONNECTION_LOST, unknown, safe_to_retry false, inspect_device_state_before_retry, one downstream write, and zero replay writes while its gate remains closed.

fault_script.action: Unconditionally clear the release loss injector, independently prove zero held input, close its generation-13 case, restore the fixture baseline, and provision opaque-session-adapter-loss-power-control generation 14 with a new four-part binding.
fault_script.expected_effect: The drained or closed generation-13 state is retired before the independent power case starts.

fault_script.action: Arm one-shot binding replacement for only device-rpc-adapter-mid-flight-loss-jetkvm-power-control after exactly one ATX request write and before acknowledgement.
fault_script.expected_effect: The ATX mutation returns CONNECTION_LOST, unknown, safe_to_retry false, inspect_device_state_before_retry, one downstream write, and zero replay writes.

fault_script.action: Unconditionally clear the ATX loss injector; inspect and emergency-release generation 14; restore and prove the recorded ATX-observable baseline; close its case-owned browser and adapter; then restore a no-incumbent fixture for the connect case.
fault_script.expected_effect: No unknown ATX state, closed mutation gate, or prior binding reaches the independent session-connect case.

fault_script.action: Arm one-shot binding replacement for only device-rpc-adapter-mid-flight-loss-jetkvm-session-connect after its connection write begins and before ready-session publication.
fault_script.expected_effect: The connect mutation returns CONNECTION_LOST, unknown, safe_to_retry false, inspect_device_state_before_retry, and zero automatic reconnect or replay.

fault_script.action: Unconditionally clear the connect loss injector; resolve and close any ambiguous case-owned connection; restore the fixture baseline; and provision opaque-session-adapter-loss-session-reconnect generation 15 with a new four-part binding.
fault_script.expected_effect: No ambiguous connect ownership, browser, channel, adapter, or request reaches the independent reconnect case.

fault_script.action: Arm one-shot binding replacement for only device-rpc-adapter-mid-flight-loss-jetkvm-session-reconnect after its reconnect write begins and before successor publication.
fault_script.expected_effect: The reconnect mutation returns CONNECTION_LOST, unknown, safe_to_retry false, inspect_device_state_before_retry, and zero automatic reconnect or replay.

fault_script.action: Unconditionally clear the reconnect loss injector; resolve and close its attempted successor and generation-15 case; restore the fixture baseline; and provision opaque-session-adapter-loss-session-status generation 16 with a new four-part binding.
fault_script.expected_effect: No reconnect ambiguity, stale generation, browser, channel, or adapter reaches the independent session-status case.

fault_script.action: Arm one-shot binding replacement for only device-rpc-adapter-mid-flight-loss-jetkvm-session-status after its binding-checked read write and before its response.
fault_script.expected_effect: The session-status read returns explicitly stale or unknown cached facts, or CONNECTION_LOST with outcome null, safe_to_retry true, and reconnect_then_capture guidance.

fault_script.action: Unconditionally clear the session-status loss injector, close its generation-16 case-owned browser and adapter, and restore the fixture baseline.
fault_script.expected_effect: No adapter-loss injector, case-owned generation, browser, channel, or binding survives the matrix.

steps.expect: Signal, resolution, and FPS return independent source, time, age, and freshness values.
Status returns explicitly stale or unknown cached facts with independent provenance, or a CONNECTION_LOST read envelope with outcome null, safe_to_retry true, and reconnect_then_capture guidance.
CONNECTION_LOST read error with outcome null, safe_to_retry true, reconnect_then_capture guidance, one old-binding read write, zero new-binding writes, and no mutation or replay.
Independent stale cached qualification or a CONNECTION_LOST read error with outcome null, safe_to_retry true, reconnect_then_capture guidance, and zero mutation or replay.

CONNECTION_LOST with unknown outcome, safe_to_retry false, inspect_device_state_before_retry, dispatched_action_count 1, completed_action_count 0, a closed generation-10 mutation gate, and zero replay writes.

CONNECTION_LOST with unknown outcome, safe_to_retry false, inspect_device_state_before_retry, dispatched_action_count 1, completed_action_count 0, a closed generation-11 mutation gate, and zero replay writes.

CONNECTION_LOST with unknown outcome, safe_to_retry false, inspect_device_state_before_retry, dispatched_count 1, completed_count 0, thirteen suppressed suffix characters, a closed generation-12 mutation gate, and zero replay writes.
CONNECTION_LOST with unknown outcome, safe_to_retry false, inspect_device_state_before_retry, one release write, a closed generation-13 mutation gate, and zero replay writes.
CONNECTION_LOST with unknown outcome, safe_to_retry false, inspect_device_state_before_retry, exactly one ATX write, a closed generation-14 mutation gate, and zero replay writes.
CONNECTION_LOST with unknown outcome, safe_to_retry false, inspect_device_state_before_retry, no ready-session publication, and zero automatic reconnect or replay.
CONNECTION_LOST with unknown outcome, safe_to_retry false, inspect_device_state_before_retry, no successor publication, and zero automatic reconnect or replay.
Explicitly stale or unknown cached facts, or a CONNECTION_LOST read error with outcome null, safe_to_retry true, reconnect_then_capture guidance, and zero mutation or replay.

pass.assertion: Signal, resolution, and FPS retain independent sources, times, ages, and freshness while proxy streaming is absent.

Each of the ten linked tools runs in its own ready case as arm one-shot adapter loss, invoke exactly one call, then unconditionally clear, retire, restore, and rebind. Reads use CONNECTION_LOST outcome-null retry guidance or explicit cached qualification; post-write mutations use unknown, safe_to_retry false, inspect-before-retry, exact counts, closed gates, and zero replay.

A cache read after channel loss is returned only with explicit per-fact stale freshness; an unobserved fact is none, null, and unknown, and every later case uses a unique current four-part binding rather than a closed or stale generation.

evidence.source: The linked focused assertion and ordered fake, replay, or live event record.
The linked focused assertion and ordered fake, replay, or live event record.
The linked focused assertion and ordered fake, replay, or live event record.

restore.action: Invoke generation-correlated emergency input release when a session exists.
restore.assertion: No held key, button, wheel producer, macro, or paste producer remains.

restore.action: Stop and join any Reliable Paste producer.
restore.assertion: Reliable Paste is inactive and its correlated producer is joined.

restore.action: Verify the fixture reports zero held keys and buttons.
restore.assertion: Held keyboard and pointer state is empty.

restore.action: Close only the device session created by this story and its browser resources.
restore.assertion: No story-owned browser, channel, or device session remains.

restore.action: Clear deterministic faults and restore the recorded fixture baseline.
restore.assertion: The next story starts only after the safe baseline is verified.

restore.action: Restore the recorded ATX-observable baseline using the reviewed fixture procedure while retaining the device lease.
restore.assertion: The baseline ATX-observable state is proven before the next story starts.

privacy.rule: Persist only allowlisted opaque identifiers, sanitized versions, counts, durations, hashes, outcomes, verification, and restoration results.
